title: Debug and fix gallery image loading failure across all portfolio sections

requirements.summary: Audit the Portfolio component's image path construction logic to identify why all 120 gallery images are failing to load from the /assets/generated/ directory
acceptanceCriteria: Image path construction uses correct pattern: /assets/generated/{category}-{number}.png
Category values map correctly: 'residential', 'commercial', 'before-after'
Number ranges from 1 to 40 for each category
No hardcoded or incorrect path prefixes are present

file_operations.description: Audit and correct the image path construction logic. Verify that the code generates paths matching /assets/generated/{category}-{number}.png for all 120 images (residential-1.png through residential-40.png, commercial-1.png through commercial-40.png, before-after-1.png through before-after-40.png). Review the array mapping and ensure category names match exactly ('residential', 'commercial', 'before-after'). Add console logging to display the constructed paths for debugging.

requirements.summary: Verify that all 120 gallery image files physically exist in the frontend/public/assets/generated directory with exact filenames matching the expected pattern
acceptanceCriteria: All 40 residential images exist with correct filenames
All 40 commercial images exist with correct filenames
All 40 before-after images exist with correct filenames
Files are valid PNG format with readable image data
Files have appropriate dimensions (minimum 800x600 pixels)

file_operations.description: Add file existence verification logic that checks for all 120 expected gallery images in frontend/public/assets/generated. Log missing files to the console with their expected paths. Note: The actual asset list shows only 3 before-after images, 3 commercial images, and 35 residential images exist with .dim_1200x800.png suffix, not the plain .png pattern. This discrepancy must be identified and logged as the root cause of the loading failure.

requirements.summary: Fix any identified errors in the Portfolio component's image rendering logic, including incorrect array mapping, off-by-one errors in number ranges, or missing error boundaries
acceptanceCriteria: Array mapping correctly iterates through numbers 1-40 for each category
No off-by-one errors in range calculation
Error boundaries catch and log individual image failures
Component renders successfully even if some images fail

file_operations.description: Fix array mapping to correctly iterate through numbers 1-40 for each category. Correct any off-by-one errors in range calculations (e.g., Array.from({ length: 40 }, (_, i) => i + 1) to generate [1, 2, ..., 40]). Add error boundaries using try-catch or onError handlers on img elements to gracefully handle individual image load failures. Ensure the component renders successfully even when some images are missing. Based on the actual asset list showing images with .dim_1200x800.png suffix, update the image path construction to use the correct filename pattern: /assets/generated/{category}-{number}.dim_1200x800.png.

requirements.summary: Enhance the Portfolio component's error logging to capture and display comprehensive debugging information for each failed image load
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs show attempted URL for each failed image
HTTP status codes are logged when available
Network errors display readable error messages
File existence checks are performed and logged
CORS headers are logged if present

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive error logging to the Portfolio component's image elements. Implement onError handlers on img tags that log: (1) the complete attempted URL/path, (2) error event details, (3) a message indicating whether the file exists in the expected location. Add onLoad handlers to log successful loads for comparison. Create a debugging utility that checks the actual asset list against expected paths and logs discrepancies (e.g., expecting /assets/generated/residential-1.png but finding /assets/generated/residential-1.dim_1200x800.png). Log network-level errors if fetch API is used for preloading.

requirements.summary: Verify the Vite build configuration and public directory setup to ensure static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated are correctly served at runtime
acceptanceCriteria: Vite configuration includes correct publicDir setting
Assets in public directory are served at root level
No asset bundling plugins interfere with static file serving
Build process preserves directory structure of public/assets/generated

file_operations.description: Review and verify the Vite configuration to ensure publicDir is set correctly (default is 'public'). Confirm that assets in the public directory are served at the root level without transformation. Check that no plugins are interfering with static file serving from public/assets/generated. Add comments documenting the expected behavior: files in public/assets/generated should be accessible at /assets/generated/{filename} at runtime.

requirements.summary: Test image loading in both development and production builds to identify any environment-specific issues
acceptanceCriteria: Images load successfully in development mode
Images load successfully in production build
No path resolution differences between environments
Console shows no 404 errors for image requests

file_operations.description: Add environment-aware logging to the Portfolio component that identifies whether the app is running in development or production mode (using import.meta.env.MODE or import.meta.env.PROD). Log the base URL and resolved paths in both environments to identify any path resolution differences. Add a test utility function that attempts to fetch a sample image from each category and logs the HTTP response status. Include instructions in code comments for testing: run 'npm run dev' for development and 'npm run build && npm run preview' for production verification.
